symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe unilateral (left) pelvic pain triggered by exertion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Abrupt exertional unilateral pelvic pain is the hallmark of torsion, often precipitated by movement.
Cyst events also present with sudden activity-related unilateral pelvic pain.
Unilateral pelvic pain in a reproductive-age woman requires ectopic to be excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain that began intermittent/colicky and became constant and severe

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intermittent then constant pain fits intermittent torsion progressing to sustained occlusion of the pedicle.
Intense visceral ischaemic pain drives prominent reflex vomiting — a classic accompaniment of torsion.
Vomiting is non-specific across the acute abdomen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain worsened by movement / positional change

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positional exacerbation is consistent with a mobile, congested adnexal mass on a twisted pedicle.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever and no preceding systemic illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lack of fever and subacute prodrome argues against an infective/inflammatory pelvic cause.
Absence of fever and a preceding periumbilical ache makes appendicitis less likely, though not excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular menstrual history, no abnormal bleeding, pregnancy not expected

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Regular menses and no abnormal bleeding lower ectopic probability, but only a pregnancy test can exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haematuria and no flank/groin radiation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anterior pelvic localisation without flank-to-groin radiation or haematuria makes ureteric colic unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localized guarding and rigidity with marked tenderness in the left iliac fossa

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised peritonism over the affected adnexa — a clinician examination finding, sealed and unavailable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tender palpable left adnexal mass on bimanual examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A tender adnexal mass strongly supports torsion — a bimanual finding that cannot be obtained via telehealth.
Pain-driven tachycardia — a clinician-measured vital sign, sealed; the AI infers distress from the reported racing heart, not a measured rate.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Failure to exclude ectopic pregnancy in a reproductive-age woman with acute unilateral pelvic pain.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative pregnancy test effectively excludes ectopic pregnancy as the cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transvaginal ultrasound — asymmetrically enlarged ovary >4 cm with absent/decreased Doppler flow

investigation_details.expected_result: Enlarged left ovary >4 cm with reduced or absent arterial and venous Doppler flow
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Torsion not confirmed and detorsion delayed — the ovary is lost to ischaemia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent/decreased ovarian Doppler flow with an enlarged ovary is the confirmatory imaging finding — a hospital investigation, never available via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden unilateral pelvic pain, recurrent vomiting, a tender adnexal mass and an enlarged ovary with absent Doppler flow together form the classic torsion picture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Mandatory first step in any reproductive-age woman with acute pelvic pain to exclude ectopic pregnancy.
The key confirmatory imaging for torsion; must be arranged urgently through the emergency department.
Ultrasound is the appropriate first-line imaging for suspected torsion; routine CT adds radiation and delay and should not be the AI's default recommendation.
